title: Rule
description: One contestable criterion of a good digital product brief. The LLM interprets `criteria` + examples and returns a verdict; it never sees `weight`, `severity`, `gate`, or `references`. Those steer the score and the gate, applied in code.

weight.description: Code-side only. Never shown to the model.

severity.description: Code-side only. Metadata/sorting; the gate (not a cap) enforces hard requirements.

references.description: Verifiable sources, authored and reviewed in the PR — not generated by the model. Shown in the report; never sent to the judge.

tier.description: standard > practice > encyclopedic. encyclopedic may not be the sole anchor of a normative claim.

locator.description: The specific clause/concept relied on. Exact version pinned in PR.

gate.description: If present, this rule is a hard publication requirement. 'not_fail' = status must be pass or partial; 'pass' = status must be pass. Enforced in code, never shown to the model.
